name: MuDi
description: 2HP active clock multiple, trigger conditioner and divider · three buffered 10V copies plus 1/2, 1/4, 1/8, 1/16 divisions · TRS input with clock on tip and reset on ring · accepts any pulse over 2V.

controls.name: (no panel controls)
controls.description: MuDi has no knobs, switches or buttons. Behaviour is fully determined by the incoming clock and reset.
controls.detail: all logic is firmware-free active buffering + divide-by-2 chain

inputs.name: CLK/RST
inputs.description: TRS combo jack. Tip carries the clock; ring carries reset. With a mono cable only the clock side is used. Any pulse above 2V is accepted and conditioned; audio-rate square waves produce suboctaves on the division outs.
inputs.normalizedTo: reset line tied low when mono cable is used

outputs.name: F (×3)
outputs.description: Three buffered conditioned copies of the input at the same frequency, each rescaled to a clean 10V pulse. Active topology — hot-patching does not drop the master clock.

outputs.name: 1/2F
outputs.description: Half the input frequency. Toggles every rising edge of CLK.

outputs.name: 1/16F
outputs.description: One sixteenth the input frequency. Good for long bar-level pulses or slow triggers.

behaviors.name: Active clock multiple
behaviors.trigger: patching multiple destinations from the three F outputs
behaviors.description: Unlike a passive mult, MuDi buffers every output. Patching and unpatching cables on the fly does not disturb the timing at other outputs — clocks stay locked while you reroute the system.

behaviors.name: Trigger conditioner
behaviors.trigger: weak or non-square pulse at CLK input
behaviors.description: The internal comparator fires on any signal crossing 2V and emits a clean 10V pulse at every output. Use MuDi to rescue low-voltage drum-machine clicks, MIDI-clock LFO pulses or rounded LFO peaks into rack-clean clocks.

behaviors.name: Binary divider chain
behaviors.description: Divisions are a flip-flop cascade — 1/2, 1/4, 1/8, 1/16 — each toggling on the rising edge of the one above. Duty cycle at each division is 50% regardless of the input duty cycle.

behaviors.name: Reset via TRS ring
behaviors.trigger: rising edge on the ring contact of the CLK/RST jack
behaviors.description: Resets the divider chain to its initial state so all divisions align with the next clock. Supplied splitter cable breaks out a second 3.5mm jack (red) for the reset. With a mono cable the reset is grounded and never fires.

behaviors.name: Audio-rate suboctaves
behaviors.description: Because the divider is logic-fast, a square at audio rate produces −1, −2, −3, −4 octave suboctaves at the division outs. The F outs pass the original square through the conditioner — useful as a hard-edged squarer before a mixer.

patchIdeas.name: Master clock distribution
patchIdeas.patch: Master clock → CLK/RST · three F outs feed sequencer clock, drum clock, modulation trigger source · 1/2F drives snare on the offbeat · 1/4F drives a slow arpeggiator · 2HP of clock routing without a dedicated hub
patchIdeas.diagram:
MuDi:

Clock Source:
Sequencer:
Drums:
LFO Trig:
Snare:
Arp:

- Clock Source (Out) c> MuDi (CLK/RST)
- MuDi (F 1) c> Sequencer (Clock)
- MuDi (F 2) c> Drums (Clock)
- MuDi (F 3) c> LFO Trig (Trig)
- MuDi (1/2F) c> Snare (Trig)
- MuDi (1/4F) c> Arp (Clock)

patchIdeas.name: Square-wave suboctave generator
patchIdeas.patch: VCO square → CLK/RST · F out passes the squared original · 1/2F = one octave down · 1/4F = two · 1/8F = three · mix all into a stack · 4-octave organ-style bass from one oscillator
patchIdeas.diagram:
MuDi:

VCO:
Mixer:

- VCO (Square) -> MuDi (CLK/RST)
- MuDi (F 1) -> Mixer (In 1) // root
- MuDi (1/2F) -> Mixer (In 2) // -1 oct
- MuDi (1/4F) -> Mixer (In 3) // -2 oct
- MuDi (1/8F) -> Mixer (In 4) // -3 oct

patchIdeas.name: Phrase-length trigger
patchIdeas.patch: Pam's or master clock at 16 PPQN → CLK/RST · 1/16F out fires once per bar at that resolution · use to trigger a chord-change envelope or reset a long sequencer · saves a divider/euclidean module when you just need a slow pulse
patchIdeas.diagram:
MuDi:

Clock:
Chord Envelope:
Long Sequencer:

- Clock (Out) c> MuDi (CLK/RST)
- MuDi (1/16F) t> Chord Envelope (Trig)
- MuDi (1/16F) t> Long Sequencer (Reset)

patchIdeas.name: MIDI clock cleanup
patchIdeas.patch: MIDI-to-CV module clock pulse (often low-voltage or rounded) → CLK/RST · MuDi rescales every output to 10V · F outs distribute clean rack clocks · divisions now reliably trigger envelope modules that previously missed quiet edges
patchIdeas.diagram:
MuDi:

MIDI Interface:
Envelope 1:
Envelope 2:
Sequencer:

- MIDI Interface (Clock) c> MuDi (CLK/RST)
- MuDi (F 1) c> Sequencer (Clock)
- MuDi (1/2F) t> Envelope 1 (Trig)
- MuDi (1/4F) t> Envelope 2 (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Reset-locked polyrhythm
patchIdeas.patch: Clock → tip of CLK/RST via splitter · bar-length reset → ring · three division outs drive separate voices · reset pulls every division back to phase zero each bar so polyrhythms repeat identically instead of drifting
patchIdeas.diagram:
MuDi:

Clock:
Bar Reset:
Voice 1:
Voice 2:
Voice 3:

- Clock (Out) c> MuDi (CLK/RST) // tip via splitter
- Bar Reset (Out) t> MuDi (CLK/RST) // ring via splitter
- MuDi (1/2F) t> Voice 1 (Trig)
- MuDi (1/4F) t> Voice 2 (Trig)
- MuDi (1/8F) t> Voice 3 (Trig)
